title.value: OTC: Optimal Tool Calls via Reinforcement Learning
title.evidence: Tool-integrated reasoning (TIR) augments large language models (LLMs) with the ability to invoke external tools...

authors.evidence: Hongru Wangα, Cheng Qianβ, Wanjun Zhongδ, Xiusi Chenβ, Jiahao Qiuσ, Shijue Huangµ, Bowen Jinβ, Mengdi Wangσ, Kam-Fai Wongα, Heng Jiβ

value: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
affiliations.evidence: αThe Chinese University of Hong Kong, βUniversity of Illinois Urbana-Champaign σPrinceton University, $^ \delta \mathsf { S u n }$ Yat-sen University, $\mu$ Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

current_issues.value: Existing reinforcement learning methods for tool invocation often overlook efficiency and cost, leading to excessive tool calls and computational overhead.
current_issues.evidence: While reinforcement learning (RL) has shown promise in improving TIR by optimizing final answer correctness, existing approaches often overlook the efficiency and cost associated with tool usage.

target.value: To develop a framework that optimizes both correctness and efficiency in tool usage within tool-integrated reasoning.
target.evidence: We aim to develop such a method based on a foundational assumption: for each question and each model, there exists an optimal number of tool calls...

framework_innovation.value: Introduction of Optimal Tool Call-controlled Policy Optimization (OTC-PO), which optimizes tool usage efficiency in reinforcement learning.
framework_innovation.evidence: We propose Optimal Tool Call-controlled Policy Optimization (OTC-PO), a simple yet effective RL-based method that enables large language models (LLMs) to learn precise and adaptive control over external tool usage.
framework_innovation.notes: OTC-PO is designed to reduce tool calls while maintaining accuracy.

metric_innovation.value: Introduction of tool productivity as a metric to measure the effectiveness and efficiency of tool calls.
metric_innovation.evidence: We additionally define a new metric, tool productivity (TP), which measures the effectiveness and efficiency of tool calls during inference.
metric_innovation.notes: TP reflects the number of correct answers per tool call.

algorithm_innovation.value: Implementation of OTC-PPO and OTC-GRPO algorithms to enhance tool call efficiency in language models.
algorithm_innovation.evidence: We instantiate OTC-PO in two variants: OTC-PPO and OTC-GRPO, tailored to different underlying RL algorithms.
algorithm_innovation.notes: These algorithms adapt existing RL methods to optimize tool usage.

performance_improvement.value: Reduction in tool calls by up to 73.1% and improvement in tool productivity by up to 229.4% without sacrificing accuracy.
performance_improvement.evidence: Experiments with Qwen-2.5 and Qwen-Math across multiple QA benchmarks show that our approach reduces tool calls by up to 73.1% and improves tool productivity by up to 229.4%...
performance_improvement.notes: Performance metrics indicate significant gains in efficiency.

tool_call_reduction.value: OTC-PO significantly reduces the number of tool calls needed to achieve correct answers.
tool_call_reduction.evidence: OTC-PPO achieves significant improvement in terms of TC and TP compared with Search-R1...
tool_call_reduction.notes: This indicates enhanced efficiency in tool usage.

model_size_effect.value: Larger language models tend to over-rely on external tools unless explicitly penalized for excessive usage.
model_size_effect.evidence: It is found that our method will not sacrifice the accuracy too much especially for larger LLMs...
model_size_effect.notes: This highlights the need for careful management of tool usage.

generalization_performance.value: OTC-PO maintains comparable accuracy while reducing tool calls across various benchmarks.
generalization_performance.evidence: OTC-PPO outperforms the Search-R1-PPO in terms of EM on all benchmarks with Qwen2.5-7B-Base model with much less tool calls...
generalization_performance.notes: This suggests robustness in performance across different tasks.

case_study_insights.value: Case studies reveal that OTC-PO can provide correct answers with fewer tool calls compared to traditional methods.
case_study_insights.evidence: OTC-PPO utilizes more accurate search by generating information-rich query...
case_study_insights.notes: This emphasizes the efficiency of the proposed method.

task_definition.value: The task is to generate correct answers with minimal tool calls in a tool-integrated reasoning setting.
task_definition.evidence: The objective of the task is to generate the correct answer $\hat { a }$ with the minimal cost of tools...

base_reward.value: Reward design focuses on correctness of answers while considering tool usage efficiency.
base_reward.evidence: Instead of only considering the correctness of the answer, we argue it is important to consider the effectiveness and efficiency of tool-integrated reasoning trajectory.

efficiency_reward.value: The efficiency reward is designed to penalize excessive tool usage, encouraging minimal tool calls.
efficiency_reward.evidence: Among trajectories, those requiring more tool calls will receive lower rewards than those achieved with fewer tool calls.

final_reward.value: The final reward combines correctness and efficiency, promoting optimal tool use.
final_reward.evidence: We regard the tool-integrated reward $r _ { t o o l }$ as a coefficient of conventional reward function...

algorithm_integration.value: OTC-PO can be integrated with existing RL algorithms like PPO and GRPO with minimal changes.
algorithm_integration.evidence: OTC-PO is lightweight and broadly applicable, requiring only minimal changes to standard RL pipelines...

datasets.value: Utilized datasets include NQ and HotpotQA for search tasks and datasets from ToRL for code tasks.
datasets.evidence: We mainly follow the Search-R1 and ToRL setting and use same baselines and datasets for the fair and comprehensive evaluation.

models.value: Models tested include Qwen-2.5-3B/7B-Base and Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B/7B-Base.
models.evidence: Using multiple LLMs—including Qwen-2.5-3B/7B-Base and Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B/7B-Base...

parameters.value: Parameters were set according to the original Search-R1 and ToRL configurations.
parameters.evidence: We re-use the same parameter in Search-R1 and ToRL respectively.

resources.value: Experiments were conducted on 8 A100-80G GPUs.
resources.evidence: We conduct our experiments on 8 A100-80G GPU...

search_tool_results.value: OTC-PPO and OTC-GRPO showed significant improvements in tool call efficiency and productivity.
search_tool_results.evidence: OTC-PPO achieves significant improvement in terms of TC and TP compared with Search-R1...

code_tool_results.value: Preliminary results indicate similar findings for code tasks as observed in search tasks.
code_tool_results.evidence: We already observe similar findings as mentioned above and will release more results soon.

behavior_analysis.value: Analysis of tool usage behavior indicates that OTC-PO reduces unnecessary tool calls significantly.
behavior_analysis.evidence: OTC-PPO utilizes more accurate search by generating information-rich query...
